title: Lydgatiana
summary: The repository XML copies the University of Michigan CME encoding of Henry Noble MacCracken's five serial Lydgatiana installments (1911-1913). Those installments edit twenty-six distinct Middle English verse texts from fifteen direct manuscript witnesses, sometimes with collateral witnesses. This record keeps the repository artifact, U-M encoding, five articles, serial-volume scans, physical manuscripts, institutional facsimiles, and DIMEV descriptions separate and records work folios inside every cached complete manuscript facsimile.

subject.description: Twenty-seven records representing twenty-six historical textual units; the Barnet selection has separate song and quatrain records

object.description: The twenty-six medieval verse units, excluding MacCracken's modern introductions

rights.component: The twenty-six medieval Middle English texts represented in the XML, excluding modern editorial matter, encoding, catalog metadata, and manuscript photography
rights.basis: The medieval texts and their fifteenth-century witness copies predate modern copyright terms. This does not determine rights in MacCracken's edition, U-M markup, modern descriptions, photographs, or provider databases.

rights.component: MacCracken's 1911-1913 article texts and editorial matter as represented in CME00109
rights.basis: All five installments were published before 1929 and are in the United States public domain by publication date.

rights.component: MacCracken's editorial introductions, notes, selection, and other authorial matter
rights.jurisdiction: Jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years or other non-US terms
rights.basis: MacCracken died in 1970, so authorial matter may remain protected through 2040 in life-plus-70 jurisdictions even though the publications are public domain in the United States. Country-specific rules and exceptions require separate assessment.

rights.component: U-M CME electronic text, markup, and collection presentation
rights.basis: The CME collection carries a No Copyright - United States statement, but item-level application to every markup and metadata component should be confirmed before redistribution.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files for all five public-domain Archiv volumes
rights.basis: The printed source articles are public domain in the United States, but Internet Archive does not guarantee uploader-provided copyright metadata and the scan files are not treated here as carrying an affirmative open license.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 data and metadata release
rights.basis: The versioned Zenodo/GitHub release identifies its data as Creative Commons Attribution 4.0.

The exact IIIF manifest supplies no license. Huntington does not require permission to publish public-domain material for which it owns no copyright and asks users to cite the item, but the researcher remains responsible and no open image license is inferred.
notes: Keep the complete cache Git-ignored and ask for clarification before redistributing the image bundle, especially in commercial print-on-demand contexts.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider license and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.basis: The exact selected-image IIIF manifest applies CC BY-NC 4.0.

notes: Commercial print-on-demand or image redistribution outside the license requires separate authorization.

rights.component: Newly supplied British Library, Bodleian, CUL, or Leiden manuscript reproductions not already covered by an exact public license
rights.jurisdiction: Repository supply terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: Access to a physical public-domain manuscript does not itself authorize redistribution of newly supplied photography. Order terms, publication permission, and any commercial-use license must be obtained for the exact delivery.

editorial_practices.representation: One CME item with five DIV1 units
editorial_practices.description: U-M collates MacCracken's five Lydgatiana installments into a single electronic compilation. This digital boundary is not evidence that the twenty-six medieval poems form one medieval work.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Many independent manuscript poems and historical Lydgate attributions
editorial_practices.representation: Fourteen short religious poems selected under the Lydgatiana title
editorial_practices.description: MacCracken's organizing title and stylistic comparisons do not make Lydgate author of the selected poems; item-level authorship and witness boundaries remain separate.
editorial_practices.scope: Installments IV-V and the compilation title more broadly

editorial_practices.source_feature: Tanner base copies and later Findern copies
editorial_practices.representation: Tanner reading text with Findern variants and comparison
editorial_practices.description: The edition uses Tanner 346 as the direct base for both ballades and collates the Findern manuscript; Findern is not substituted as the continuous base.

editorial_practices.representation: Italic type in MacCracken and inherited emphasis in CME
editorial_practices.description: MacCracken states or demonstrates that italics can mark expanded manuscript abbreviations. The exact appearance and scope of an expansion must therefore be checked against the manuscript image; italics are not a facsimile of the omitted strokes.
editorial_practices.scope: Across the five installments where italicized expansions occur

editorial_practices.source_feature: Competing old/current foliation and CUDL display labels in Ff.1.6
editorial_practices.representation: MacCracken/provider and current DIMEV locators retained together
editorial_practices.description: Direct image inspection resolves the Prayer to Christ's Name at CUDL canvas 252 (provider label 124v; current DIMEV f. 146v). Canvas 296, although provider-labeled 146v, contains a different poem. The ballades similarly require current-to-provider foliation crosswalks.
editorial_practices.scope: Lydgatiana II ballades and V.2 Prayer to Christ's Name

editorial_practices.source_feature: Unique verse witness lacking approximately its opening hundred lines
editorial_practices.representation: An 859-line acephalous reading text beginning within the journey narrative
editorial_practices.description: The edition and encoding preserve the surviving fragment; their first line is not the work's original beginning.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Cotton Caligula A II and a poorer Ashmole 59 copy of Five Joys
editorial_practices.description: MacCracken explicitly prefers the Cotton witness and rejects the poorer Ashmole text as a base; Ashmole is collateral, not the direct source represented in CME00109.
editorial_practices.scope: Lydgatiana V.9, Five Joys of Mary

editorial_practices.representation: Harley 2250 in V.10 and an encoded [fol.168vo] milestone for Arundel 168
editorial_practices.description: Contemporary catalog evidence and DIMEV resolve Maria Virgo Assumpta Est to Harley 2251, ff. 33r-34r. John Marion's ABC is at Arundel 168 f. 1ra-vb; '168v' confuses the shelfmark with a folio. Neither error creates another witness or passage.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00109.xml.
evidence.locator: IDG, TEMPHEAD, TEXT and complete BODY
evidence.summary: Direct local evidence for repository identity, five-installment structure, twenty-six historical units, headings, notes, printed pagination, encoded manuscript milestones, lineation, and typography.

evidence.citation: Structural audit of CME00109.xml against its checked-in bytes.
evidence.summary: Confirms 266,734 bytes; five DIV1, 31 DIV2, nine DIV3, 3,509 L, 491 LG, 53 P, 80 PB, and 125 NOTE elements. Of the L elements, 3,505 belong to twenty-six historical poems and four quote verse in modern introductions.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00109.
evidence.summary: Official identity and public presentation of the electronic compilation; the checked-in XML independently grounds structural claims.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English collection information and rights statement.
evidence.summary: Supports the collection-level No Copyright - United States assertion while leaving item/component-specific applicability and provider terms to be confirmed.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, 'Lydgatiana' I-V, Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 126-127, 129-131 (1911-1913).
evidence.summary: Grouped citation for the five immediate source editions. Dedicated evidence records and complete cached volume scans establish each installment's exact pages, witnesses, editorial claims, and article date.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, 'Lydgatiana. I. The Life of Holy Job,' Archiv 126 (1911), 365-370, in complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Immediate source edition for installment I; supports HM 140/former Phillipps 8299, ff. 93v-96v, selection, 181-line text, and editorial practice.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, 'Lydgatiana. II. Two Chaucerian Ballades,' Archiv 127 (1911), 323-327, in complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Immediate source edition for installment II; supports Tanner base passages, Findern collation, article title, lineation, and dating claims.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, 'Lydgatiana. III. The Three Kings of Cologne,' Archiv 129 (1912), 50-68, in complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Immediate source edition for installment III; supports Add MS 31042 locus, acephalous 859-line verse text, and MacCracken's cautious attribution and regional hypotheses.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, 'Lydgatiana. IV,' Archiv 130 (1913), 286-311, in complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Immediate source edition for installment IV; supports all eight Trinity selections, loci, lineation, and rejection of John Stow's added Lydgate attributions.

evidence.citation: Henry Noble MacCracken, 'Lydgatiana. V. Fourteen Short Religious Poems,' Archiv 131 (1913), 40-63, in complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Immediate source edition for installment V; supports the fourteen selections, stated base witnesses, Cotton-over-Ashmole choice, headings, lineation, and inherited locator errors.

evidence.citation: Huntington Library catalog and complete IIIF manifest for HM 140.
evidence.locator: 353 canvases; Life of Holy Job ff. 93v-96v, canvases 192-198
evidence.summary: Official identity, historical Phillipps number, 1475-1500 date, physical extent, complete image sequence, exact work canvases, and absence of an object-level license.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that the Library does not require permission to quote or publish public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, assigns rights investigation to the researcher, and asks for specific item citation.

evidence.citation: ARLIMA, 'The life of holy Job,' record 17998.
evidence.summary: Supports title, fifteenth-century date, 181-line twenty-six-stanza form, unique HM 140 locus, incipit, explicit, and MacCracken bibliography.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries catalog and complete IIIF manifest for MS Tanner 346.
evidence.summary: Official shelfmark, complete digital sequence, exact source passages, canvas mappings, Bodleian attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library complete object and IIIF manifest for MS Ff.1.6.
evidence.locator: 329 canvases; ballades canvases 25-30; Prayer to Christ's Name canvas 252
evidence.summary: Complete provider object for the Findern manuscript, direct image labels and canvases, provider rights statements, and the data needed to preserve old/current foliation crosswalks.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of cached Ff.1.6 IIIF images at canvases 25-30, 252, and 296.
evidence.summary: Confirms the ballade boundaries and that the Prayer to Christ's Name is on canvas 252 under provider label 124v/current DIMEV f. 146v; provider canvas 296 labeled 146v contains a different text.

evidence.citation: Frank Schaer, The Three Kings of Cologne: manuscript tradition and Middle English versions (PhD thesis, University of Adelaide, 1992).
evidence.locator: discussion of the unique verse version and Add MS 31042
evidence.summary: Supports the unique, acephalous verse-work boundary, relation to the John of Hildesheim tradition, approximate first-half-fifteenth-century dating, and cautious treatment of northern/Scottish origin and Lydgate authorship.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge catalog and complete IIIF manifest for MS R.3.21.
evidence.locator: 686 canvases; CME00109 passages at canvases 354-360, 398-399, 447-465, 494, and 554-558
evidence.summary: Official shelfmark, manuscript description, complete provider image sequence, exact passage mapping, item-level CC BY-NC 4.0 license, and a verified local bundle containing all 686 inventoried image members.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library complete object and IIIF manifest for MS Kk.1.6.
evidence.locator: 505 canvases; Have Mercy canvases 399-400; Regina Celi canvases 408-409
evidence.summary: Complete provider object, exact passages, canvas mappings, downloadable-image CC BY-NC 3.0 terms, zoom-image reservation, and separate metadata terms.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue records for Add MS 31042, Harley MS 2251, Add MS 34360, Arundel MSS 249 and 168, and Cotton MS Caligula A II.
evidence.summary: Official current shelfmarks and manuscript-description routes for the six direct BL witnesses. No complete public facsimile of these exact target objects was confirmed in this review.

evidence.citation: British Library manuscript access, digitisation, enquiry, and publication-licensing routes.
evidence.summary: Current routes to ask whether an existing surrogate exists, request a physical item or digitisation quotation, obtain the current price list, and clarify publication or commercial reuse separately.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records for the twenty-six CME00109 historical textual units.
evidence.summary: Versioned CC BY 4.0 evidence for work boundaries, incipits, forms, subjects, witness loci, collateral witnesses, current foliation, and the Harley 2251 correction.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian selected-image IIIF objects for Rawlinson C.48, Douce 326, and Rawlinson poet. 34.
evidence.locator: Rawl. C.48 f. 5r only; Douce 326 f. 4v only; Rawl. poet. 34 ff. 10v and 19r
evidence.summary: Exact partial image objects and CC BY-NC 4.0 statements. The first two have no target-work overlap; Rawlinson poet. 34 overlaps only at f. 19r within target ff. 18v-20r.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Medieval Manuscripts catalog TEI for MS Douce 326 and MS Rawlinson poet. 34, fixed at commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.summary: Version-fixed official catalog data for current shelfmarks, ARKs, contents, and physical descriptions where the current public catalog interface did not expose a stable equivalent page.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, ordering and reproducing special-collections images.
evidence.summary: Current route for requesting complete or additional manuscript images and for clarifying delivery, attribution, noncommercial, publication, and commercial licensing terms.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Explains item-level CC BY-NC 4.0 licensing and the distinction between noncommercial and commercial reuse.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library image-supply and reproduction terms, together with exact CUDL object statements.
evidence.summary: Supports the distinction between public CUDL downloadable-image licenses, all-rights-reserved zoom images, fee-based new supply, and separate permission for publication or commercial reuse.

evidence.citation: Leiden University Libraries, Special Collections access and services.
evidence.summary: Official enquiry route for Vossius Germ. Gall. Q.9 when no stable public object-level facsimile or catalog page could be confirmed; exact reproduction and reuse terms require a repository response.

evidence.citation: Official catalog and digital-object records for the fifteen direct manuscript witnesses described separately in this manifest.
evidence.summary: Grouped evidence for physical survival, holdings, shelfmarks, and the separation of physical manuscripts from editions and digital surrogates; exact object evidence is cited in dedicated records.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official overview supporting United States public-domain treatment of the 1911-1913 publications while not deciding rights in later markup, photography, or other jurisdictions.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, Rights information guidance.
evidence.summary: Explains that Internet Archive does not guarantee item copyright information; item metadata is a provider assertion rather than a warranty or license.

open_questions.question: Which anonymous Lydgatiana poems, if any, can be assigned securely to John Lydgate or another identifiable author beyond John Marion?
next_steps: Review post-DIMEV attribution scholarship item by item.
Treat Stow's added names, MacCracken's stylistic comparisons, and manuscript rubrics as scoped evidence rather than blanket authorship.
Preserve John Marion's signed ABC as the only currently confirmed named authorship in the selection.

open_questions.question: What exact textual route, date, and region produced the unique verse Three Kings of Cologne?
next_steps: Compare Add MS 31042 directly with the Latin and Middle English prose traditions.
Reassess northern and possible Scottish linguistic claims using current scholarship.
Do not turn stylistic proximity to Lydgate into an attribution.

open_questions.question: Can all legacy/current foliation and shelfmark conflicts be confirmed from institutional collation statements?
next_steps: Ask CUL to document the Findern old/current foliation concordance used by CUDL and DIMEV.
Confirm Arundel 249 and Cotton Caligula A II boundaries against new images.
Treat Harley 2250 in V.10 and the Arundel 168 'fol. 168v' milestone as editorial errors unless contrary primary evidence appears.

open_questions.question: Can complete reusable whole-manuscript facsimiles be obtained for the British Library and Leiden witnesses and the three only-partially-imaged Bodleian witnesses?
next_steps: Ask first whether an existing preservation surrogate can be delivered; request the complete current physical codex rather than only target folios when feasible.
In every request identify the target work folios separately so the full object can be mapped back to CME00109.
Obtain written repository-display, scholarly-publication, commercial print-on-demand, and redistribution terms for the exact delivery.

open_questions.question: Which cached manuscript image bundles may be redistributed through a repository that also supports commercial print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Keep source-cache files Git-ignored and publish URLs, checksums, download status, and work locators rather than cached image bytes by default.
Treat Bodleian, CUDL, and Trinity CC BY-NC images as noncommercial and obtain separate permission before any commercial reuse.
Seek item-specific clarification from Huntington and exact written terms for any newly supplied BL, Bodleian, CUL, or Leiden images.

review_notes: The cataloging subject is MacCracken's/U-M's edited compilation boundary. It must not be presented as one medieval work or as wholly authored by Lydgate.

Complete manuscript facsimile means the complete sequence exposed by the provider's IIIF manifest, not proof that omitted physical surfaces do not exist. Every complete local bundle includes a work_portion mapping.

The full five source serial volumes, full HM 140, Tanner 346, Ff.1.6, Trinity R.3.21, and Kk.1.6 provider objects, three Bodleian selected-image objects, and DIMEV v1.0 are cached under gitignored source-cache/CME00109 with exact source URLs, byte counts, and checksums.
Public access does not imply permission for redistribution or commercial print-on-demand. The manifest deliberately separates access status, copyright assessment, exact licenses, and provider terms.

DIMEV has twenty-seven relevant records because the Battle of Barnet selection comprises a song and a separate following quatrain; the metadata manifest retains twenty-six historical textual units at the edited-compilation level.
Internet Archive's generic metadata dates for these serial volumes were not used; publication years come from inspected title pages and MacCracken's printed articles.
